data.title: Hick's Law in Action
data.description: Simplifies decision-making by reducing complexity in UI design.
data.technical_spec: Interfaces are streamlined, focusing on essential features to minimize cognitive load.

data.title: Gestalt Principle of Proximity
data.description: Groups related elements together to form a coherent user experience.
data.technical_spec: Leveraged in chat interfaces, where messages, timestamps, and user actions are closely placed to enhance readability.

data.title: Fitts's Law for Interaction Design
data.description: Optimizes touch targets to enhance usability, especially on mobile devices.
data.technical_spec: Critical interactive elements are sufficiently large and appropriately spaced for ease of use.

data.title: The Aesthetic-Usability Effect
data.description: Aesthetically pleasing interfaces are easier to use and improve user satisfaction.
data.technical_spec: Uses a minimalistic design approach with a consistent color palette that aligns with the brand.

data.title: Miller’s Law in Information Presentation
data.description: Limits user exposure to just the necessary amount of information at one time to reduce overload.
data.technical_spec: Information and notifications are chunked, and additional data is shown on demand.
